problem:
The line $y = 2x + 7$ is to be parameterized using vectors.  Which of the following options are valid parameterizations?

(A) $\begin{pmatrix} x \\ y \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 0 \\ 7 \end{pmatrix} + t \begin{pmatrix} 2 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix}$

(B) $\begin{pmatrix} x \\ y \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} -7/2 \\ 0 \end{pmatrix} + t \begin{pmatrix} -1 \\ -2 \end{pmatrix}$

(C) $\begin{pmatrix} x \\ y \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix}  1 \\ 9 \end{pmatrix} + t \begin{pmatrix} 6 \\ 3 \end{pmatrix}$

(D) $\begin{pmatrix} x \\ y \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 2 \\ -1 \end{pmatrix} + t \begin{pmatrix} 1/2 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix}$

(E) $\begin{pmatrix} x \\ y \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} -7 \\ -7 \end{pmatrix} + t \begin{pmatrix} 1/10 \\ 1/5 \end{pmatrix}$

Enter the letters of the correct options, separated by commas.

classified_questions.question: What is the definition of parameterizing a line using vectors?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can we find a point on the line $y = 2x + 7$? If so, then what would be its corresponding vector representation?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can we find a direction vector for the line using the given point and the equation of the line?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can we represent the line using the parameterization form $\begin{pmatrix} x \\ y \end{pmatrix} = \mathbf{v} + t \mathbf{d}$? If so, which options satisfy this condition?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can we rule out any options that do not satisfy the condition mentioned in step 4?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can we find any other points on the line to verify if they lie on the parameterized lines from the remaining options?
classified_questions.label: b
